reviews.title: It's ok. But you can get a Fitbit style watch for less
reviews.text: I wear it near my pulse because I feel it gives you a more accurate reading. The sleep monitor sucks. It didn't start monitoring my sleep until I turned it around and put the sensors on my inner wrist

reviews.title: Sent back twice because the blood pressure measurement was inaccurate
reviews.text: I had some confidence in buying this at first.  It connected to app nicely and did well with sleep measurement when I tried that.  I also liked the face choices.  However, having a reasonably accurate blood pressure measurement as long as it is included as a feature was very important for me.  It always read about the same measurement of 140/86, but that was not accurate when compared to a cuff style.  It also read the same for my wife who is always different.  I returned one, got an easy replacement and had the same issue.  Returned for refund this time.  I did not check if waterproof, but that was also an important feature.

reviews.title: Changing my review
reviews.text:
Band does not clip completely into the FitBit--in fact, you do NOT get that snug ""snap" or "click" sound when you attach it. It fell off my wrist 3 times in yoga this morning. Returning this junk.

I bought these so I'd have something other than the band that came with my Fitbit Charge 5. These are okay. They look fine, but they are really, really long, with no way to shorten them. I have small wrists (5 3/4") and as you can tell from the photo, the end of the band comes almost to the watch face on the other side and sticks up. I did eventually pull the tab so that it lays flat, but I'm not used to having the end of the watch band on the side. The product description online says it fits a wrist 5.1" to 8.7". If my wrist was any smaller, the band would probably cover the watch face. Unfortunately, there wasn't a "small" option.

reviews.title: Good watch. Has sensory issues
reviews.text: Husband loves his Christmas gift. Only complaint we have is he has flick his wrist multiple times very hard to get it to light up. He plans to see if there’s  anything he can on his end to not have to that. It’s a great watch for the price.

reviews.title: Great watch
reviews.text: This watch did everything I needed it to do.  It keeps very accurate time and has many excellent features.  The ones I was most interested in were the sleep tracker, heart and blood pressure monitor and step counter as these  are medically necessary.  The accuracy was top of the line.  My only issue is this watch caused the area on my skin that contacted the watch to get a bad rash.  While the rash was essentially harmless, it is caused by a chemical reaction in my body.  Not the watches fault, and I've experienced this side effect with other watches in the past.  Now I use it only occasionally and for only a couple of days at a time to still track the previously mentioned condition.  It's a great watch that I would recommend.

NEW LEVEL WATCH-STYLE DIVE COMPUTER: Access advanced features for a range of diving activities - including the ability to plan dives on the device for recreational, technical, and free diving. Enjoy up to 80 hours in dive mode, 16 days in smartwatch mode and 15 hours in GPS mode with music.

Spotify integration requires separate Spotify Premium membership. GARMIN DESCENT MK2i DIVERS BUNDLE: Garmin Descent Mk2i (Titanium Carbon Gray DLC/Black Band) Dive Computer Watch, PlayBetter Waterproof Dry Bag with Window, PlayBetter Waterproof Cell Phone Pouch, Extra-Long Adjustment Strap & USB Charging Cable

reviews.title: Excellent Dive Computer and Smart Watch
reviews.text:
I have been wearing this for a solid 4 months, using it to track dives, swimming, running, walking, treadmill workouts, etc. In watch mode the battery lasts a long time, but for activities you will find yourself charging it after about 5-8 hours of activity use.

I was a bit worried my skin would react as I cannot usually wear anything metal unless high quality gold or sterling silver. No problems with this watch - the band is comfortable and the watch, while a bit larger than I was used to, fits well and without any reaction.

Functions while diving were flawless. You have a lot of parameter's you can set - this thing is designed for divers. It tracks entry and exit locations accurately. I wish I could tell it when my dive is starting and when done, the automatic feature is a bit annoying in this regard. However, it is one less thing to remember when you are in the water and doing a buddy check to get going. It is pretty neat to how nicely it updates the app on your phone and makes sharing easy.

Pool swimming is not entirely accurate - the watch cannot record kickboard laps, for example, so if you like to vary your workout you won't have accurate times or distances. Basically, any time you are not completing actual strokes you won't get a record.

Treadmill work - you can adjust the settings to match your treadmill readings. I found it was not very accurate and finally quit bothering to adjust it. The watch was either lower in distance or higher every time, but close enough to just let it be. At least once you leave it alone you get consistent numbers, and you can also record numbers from the treadmill if you want.

Walking and trail running features are great - I love seeing where I was with the GPS integration. I work out regularly on a measured trail and the distances are spot-on with the MK2S. There are workouts it will suggest, but I tend to stick to my own and ignore those. I was really frustrated to find my VO2 was consistently "Poor" all fall, but have seen some improvement recently with regular workouts. Kind of cool getting readings like that.

I plan to use this watch for backpacking as well - I have not had an opportunity to test it since the purchase, but my last trip my sister had a different model and it worked well other than eating power. You absolutely have to carry one or two good battery packs to charge it. I really wish this had a solar option, which I understand is in future plans, so I will more than likely upgrade when that is offered. Even so, hiking puts your wrist in the shade a lot of the time unless you are hiking alpine locations, so not sure how well solar would actually work.

All said, I love my "toy". It helps keep me motivated when I see I have been in the office too long or my scores are a lot lower. I am competitive and happy to compete against myself, so it works well. I don't use the connections, although I have considered seeing if anyone I know has a Garmin, but so far its all me :D While pricy, for someone who loves a wide variety of athletic activity, you can't beat the Garmin Mk2 in any of its versions. My husband is still trying to decide if he wants the Mk2S or the Mk2i but he will be getting one or the other. Fantastic product.

title: Samsung Galaxy Watch3 2020 Smartwatch (Bluetooth + Wi-Fi + GPS) International Model (Bronze, 41mm)
description: The only smartwatch with a precise rotating bezel for navigating between apps. Larger screen, but lighter and thinner for more comfort: With its subtle rotating bezel, it's easier to see and focus on what's important on an even larger screen. The Galaxy Watch3 is thinner and smaller, and up to 15% lighter with a sophisticated look that, thanks to its reduced frame, is perfect for the wrist. Breathe easier with built-in oxygen measurement: The Galaxy Watch3 uses red LEDs and infrared rays to calculate your SpO2 levels or the amount of oxygen in your blood. Because oxygen saturation is directly related to physical activity, you can use the Galaxy Watch3 to push your routine to the next level. Enjoy a better sleep: The Galaxy Watch3 records your active sleep, deep sleep, and total sleep time to keep track and improve the quality of your sleep. Samsung partnered with the National Sleep Foundation to include educational content and provide tips on your device to help you sleep better. Do your best and then repeat it: With over 120 home workout programs to choose from, the Galaxy Watch3 helps you move your body at your own pace. Pick one from your phone and stream it on your TV. It will show you your heart rate in real-time while you exercise. Use the Ridge Sport strap for even more accurate stats. Continue your conversations: When you receive a new message, the Galaxy Watch3 shows your conversation history so you can follow them. You can also view images without needing an extra touch, making it easier to keep up while you're working out. Add some context with the smart answer: Respond to messages at all times and without missing a beat. The Galaxy Watch3's smart reply analyzes the texts and photos you receive and gives you possible answers in different categories, such as food, celebrations, selfies and much more.

title: SAMSUNG GEAR S3 FRONTIER Smartwatch 46MM (Bluetooth Only) - Dark Grey (Renewed)
description:
Stay in touch, without reaching for your phone, with the smartwatch that’s engineered for adventure. Convenience being the key feature, the watch offers fitness tracking, app access and watch face customization. With the Gear S3, you can pay right from your wrist and track your location as well. The Gear S3 is compatible with both Android and iOS smartphones.
Never Miss a Moment
The Gear S3 is Bluetooth connected so you can receive texts, emails, and alerts without reaching for your phone. With a built-in speaker and microphone, you can also make and answer calls hands-free. Just rotate the distinctive steel bezel to easily access apps and notifications so you can instantly check texts, updates, and more.
Pay with Your Watch
With Samsung Pay on your Gear S3, you can easily make payments with your watch almost anywhere you can swipe or tap a credit card - even without your phone or wallet.
Get Out There
Whether you’re off-roading or exploring a new city, the built-in GPS tracks your activity and makes it easy to share your location.
Designed for The Elements
The military-grade Gear S3 is tough enough to handle the elements. It resists water, dust, extreme temperatures, and the occasional drop so you can venture out with confidence.
Go for Days
Go nonstop for days on a single charge.4 When it’s time to recharge, the wireless charger lets you power up without slowing down.
Tailored to You
Make the Gear S3 your own. Customize your watch face, choosing from thousands of striking colors and designs. Plus easily swap out the standard 22mm band.
features: The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
Text, call and get notifications directly from your watch through your Bluetooth connected smartwatch.
Access the apps you use every day on your Gear S3. Catch a ride, check scores, stream music, and more.
With the built-in S Health app, track your steps, monitor your heart rate, and more.

reviews.title: Just over 90 days and it completely died
reviews.text: Rrally liked this watch at first. The battery did not last very long, even after adjusting settings. Thought it was the battery so we bought a new one, nope just completely died now. The new battery is fine, charges up and works, but the watch will not even turn on with new battery in it.

reviews.title: Love this
reviews.text: Love this watch

reviews.title: Husband LOVES This
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband for his birthday and Father's Day and he loves it. He used to have a Galaxy S9, but dropped it into a lake while we were fishing *face palm*. But he ended up getting an old S7 from a family member and the watch connects with that as well. The only complaint I have heard from him is about the band, which was easy and cheap to replace with a metal material that didn't stick to his wrist as much as the rubbery band that comes with the watch.

reviews.title: Great looking watch!

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text:
I bought this when it first came out, I needed it's walking and stair climbing metrics.

It's big, heavy, and if you're at all active, it's on the charge cradle for around 1 hour. The big, heavy part, some like it, I wish it were a little smaller. The battery life, it's an early version, I didn't expect much, my next watch, 20 day minimum.

All in all, it's a very functional watch, feature laden, and good, as long as Samsung supports the OS

reviews.title: Angry customer
reviews.text: So after receiving these items on March 29, 2021, it took 5 hrs to charge, the headphone jack doesn't even fit the watch!  On top of that my daughter plays on it for 15 mins and it dies!  She wore it to play on it and the wrist band broke!  I'm not happy at all and will never be buying this brand ever again!  It wouldn't even turn on right away after charging! The headphones are absolutely the worst thing ever ! I plugged them in to my phone and all we heard was high pitched screeching!  I'm not asking for my money back, I'm just warning others to be careful!  Complete false advertising!

reviews.text: I am so happy with the way this looks. It doesn’t look a traditional smart watch. It is more stylish. Would purchase again

reviews.title: Looks great but a pain to navigate
reviews.text: I ordered this watch because I love the style. I don’t care for the styles of most smart watches I’ve seen.  This watch looks great, very stylish!!  It comes with a tool to allow you to adjust the band length which is very helpful.  It keeps time perfectly.  It counts your steps and monitors sleep quality. The heart rate and BP monitors seem to be accurate as they are right on with my normal numbers. It measures sleep quality.  The hours I sleep is accurate but I don't know how to tell if the deep vs. light sleep is correct.  I assume its based on heartrate which seems to be accurate.  I cannot get the female app to sync, however,  I don’t use this feature. The apps are a pain to navigate through but aside from the female app they work well. The best part of this watch aside from its ascetics is that the charge lasts four to five days.

reviews.title: Great design, quality, love the hidden backside
reviews.text: These bands are great to reduce the amount of time I spent sticking the band into the holder(?) on the back side. My wrist is fairly small, so there's usually a long piece hanging off.  At first these take a little longer to get on, but once they're on you never have to mess with the band all day.  They look good, feel good and seem like they'll last for several years.  Colors are pretty much what they look like on screen.

reviews.title: Awesome narrow bands
reviews.text: I love that these are narrower than standard.  They are easy to install and remove which speaks to their careful design and craftmanship.  They have metal which touches my wrist but so far no reaction to it.

title: Garmin 010-02541-10 fenix 7X Sapphire Solar,Larger adventure smartwatch, Solar Charging Capabilities, rugged outdoor GPS watch, touchscreen, wellness features, Carbon Gray DLC Titanium with Black Band
description: There are 7 days in a week. And the fēnix® 7X Sapphire Solar multisport GPS watch is built to go strong for all of them. Featuring a big 1.4” display, built-in LED flashlight and Power Sapphire™ solar charging lens, it has the long-lasting battery life to sustain your athletic and outdoor challenges. Use trusted button controls or the new touchscreen interface to access selections. Gain performance insights and metrics that help you manage your exertion and build stamina. Optimize recovery with 24/7 health and wellness monitoring of heart rate, respiration, stress, sleep and more this device is intended to give an estimation of your activity and metrics; it is not a medical device). Outdoor navigation sensors and new multi-band GNSS technology help guide you with a higher level of positioning accuracy, even in challenging environments. Plus, you’ll have preloaded TopoActive maps and worldwide ski and golf maps right on your wrist. To complement your tamer days, connected features include smart notifications when connected to a compatible smartphone, music storage and Garmin Pay™ contactless payments in supported countries and payment networks.
features: Long-running solar powered multisport GPS watch with scratch-resistant Power Sapphire lens and always-on 1.4” display uses the sun’s energy to extend battery life; a built-in LED flashlight keeps you going after dark.Supported Application:Elevation Tracker,Distance Tracking,Fitness Tracker,Multisport Tracker,Sleep Monitor. Connectivity technology:Wi-Fi,GPS. Wireless comm standard:Bluetooth

Battery performance: in smartwatch mode, up to 28 days while indoors or up to 37 days with solar charging in 3 hours of direct sunlight (50,000 lux) per day; in GPS mode, up to 89 hours indoors or up to 122 hours with solar charging continuously in direct sunlight (50,000 lux)
Take your training to the next level with endurance and real-time stamina tracking, training status, advanced performance metrics and 30 plus built-in sports apps

Get 24/7 health and wellness monitoring with wrist-based heart rate, Pulse Ox (not available in all countries), stress and enhanced sleep tracking (this device is intended to give an estimation of your activity and metrics; it is not a medical device)

Navigate the outdoors with a higher level of positioning accuracy, thanks to new multi-band frequency and GNSS network support (GPS, GLONASS and Galileo) plus built-in sensors for 3-axis compass, gyroscope and barometric altimeter
Find freedom and adventure with preloaded TopoActive maps — plus maps for thousands of golf courses and ski resorts worldwide
Built-in Wi-Fi connectivity lets you download maps for any continent at any time — without using a computer

Enjoy everyday lifestyle connectivity features, including smart notifications when connected to a compatible smartphone, music storage and our Garmin Pay contactless payment solution in supported countries and payment networks
Trusted button controls that work in any environment are matched with a highly responsive touchscreen interface for quick, convenient access to selections

reviews.title: Pricey but worth it for multi day backpacking
reviews.text: I tried the Fénix 5X and returned it because it was very tedious to use the maps with buttons only.  The Fénix 7X Sapphire Solar not only solves this problem but has a massive increase in battery life and better sensors all around.  Most well-traveled trails don’t need fancy GPS watches, but I’ve been going off the trails more and more, and having an accurate GPS and not just a breadcrumb app is convenient. It beats relying only on Compass and maps only.

reviews.title: More watch than I need
reviews.text: I've had the 3 and 6x prior to this. I upgraded for the flash and the touchscreen as well as the significantly improved battery life. This thing is bullet proof. All the previous functionality through the Garmin app is maintained if not improved. I use it as an everyday watch and for my biking and hiking needs. I've also used it for swimming and SUP and have been impressed. As with previous versions, more faces will be developed so I'm not holding the slim options against it for now. Not cheap but it's hands down the winner in this space for me.

reviews.title: Comprehensive and accurate. Excellent fitness tracking device.
reviews.text: I use the Fenix 7 to track all  of my fitness activity, my sleep numbers, weather, text messages, and security around the house, music, etc..  It was too expensive for me but I'm glad I purchased it as it has paid for itself and has become indispensable in many ways.  I cannot imagine a better more functional fitness watch.  You will appreciate the battery life and all the other available functions.

reviews.title: A worthwhile upgrade from the 6X
reviews.text:
I just upgraded from the 6X sapphire to a 7X solar sapphire watch during Garmin's anniversary sale. It wasn't that I was unhappy with the old one, it's been invaluable, but there were just enough new features I decided it'd be worth a try. All-in-all I'm very glad that I did. I weighed the extra battery life from the larger solar array along with the ability to reuse my existing QuickFit 26 bands into my decision to keep the same size rather than go with something a bit smaller and, perhaps, sportier-looking. Fortunately I have big hands and wrists so it doesn't look overlarge.

Others have spoken about the smartwatch in more detail, so I'll both be brief and focus on the upgrade process instead of on the watch itself here. Given Garmin's otherwise clunky software, I was amazed at how easy it was to configure the new device, download a watch face, and port over my data from the old one. Even with the firmware and map upgrades it only took about 20 minutes, most of which was downloading stuff and reconnecting the new device to my wi-fi and phone app. All I needed was Garmin Express (on my laptop) and Garmin Connect (on my smartphone).

The bottom line is that the 7X looks great, works perfectly, has massively improved battery life, is easy to set up, and comes with a few extra cool features like the flashlight (which is far more useful than expected).

reviews.title: good but expenisive
reviews.text:
My old tracking watch was a suunto abit 2. I does the basic recording of your running, cycling, walking and show you the distance on the map when downloaded.

I was looking at a garmin bike gp map, like the 830 series. I just wanted to see streets and location as I bike and not use my cellphone. There aren't that many models that show real-time map, even the one from other brand that shows map, it lacks a lot of details or cross street. Garmon fenix 7 was the most rebus in the lineup in term of map.

Goods (mostly compared to my older suunto ambit 2:
+faster gps acquiring
+has heartbeat detector, so don't have to wear a chest monitor.
+fit nicely, I have skinny wrist and it is almost the same sixe as the height
+More detail on map than the regular fenix 7.
+battery is like around 2 weeks if I don't realy use the recording.
+convenient to have a map on your wrist, grant it is a tad small.
+tell me I have low Vo2.
+can monitor your sleep pattern, rem, deep sleep, etc.
+I was on the east coast and now back on the west coast and I didn't even have to adjust the time. Not sure If I had to adjust the daylight either.
+like google smartwatch it can send you alert on your screen.
+battery is great, since it has solar and doesn't have the bright and vivid oled screen of garmin epic. ( I bought mine instore and was able to compare both) I prefer the longer battery life. My smartphone can be fore more detail items.
+alot of good info, temp, alt, sun set heart rate you can see on the fly.
+touch screen works very well.

neutrals:
*has a flashlight led, I don't use it much. Actually I hard every use it.
*good little vibrate when you get alert.
*this is music and wallet stuff I also don't use.

Negatives:
-cost
-while the map is good, I might want a bigger cycling map later.
-compared to the garmin epic which has a brighter screen, this one isn't as bright, Seem reflect light so you can see even at night but not very bright.
-it is quit dim at times

I want to get the base model but looking at this at the store, i wanted the bigger screen, the solar power and sapphire. I scratched my watch from just bumping into things so anything less than sapphire is a no go. Is still might end up buying a cycling gps map, but this is good enough for now. price is just way too high when they add one feature over the next model for 100 more.

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Perfect and of high quality

reviews.title: Good Replacement Band For Fitbit Sense
reviews.text:
The AK Silicone Band attached securely to my Fitbit Sense without any problems.
It fits my wrist perfectly and is very comfortable to wear. I've had it on for approximately a month with no issues. It's not as easy to put on my wrist as the original Fitbit band, but that is a minor issue.
It appears to be very well made and time will tell about durability.
I'm very happy that I purchased this replacement band.
